title: Actionable viewer UX means fewer choices, not more labels
summary: For the Kage viewer, actionable UX should simplify the surface area instead of adding repeated Action labels. Keep Overview to a few primary decisions, keep Intel to top priority signals, cap Debug/raw rows, and avoid pe
body: For the Kage viewer, actionable UX should simplify the surface area instead of adding repeated Action labels. Keep Overview to a few primary decisions, keep Intel to top priority signals, cap Debug/raw rows, and avoid per-row action spam. Users should see one obvious next place to click rather than a dense wall of facts and instructions.

context.fact: For the Kage viewer, actionable UX should simplify the surface area instead of adding repeated Action labels. Keep Overview to a few primary decisions, keep Intel to top priority signals, cap Debug/raw rows, and avoid per-row action spam. Users should see one obvious next place to click rather than a dense wall of facts and instructions.
